diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pounding headache is the hallmark symptom of the hypertensive surge in autonomic dysreflexia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Drenching diaphoresis and flushing above the level of the lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sweating and flushing above the lesion, with vasoconstriction below, are characteristic autonomic features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive difficulty voiding with continuous painless overflow dribbling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Overflow incontinence with a failure to empty indicates a chronically retaining bladder that has acutely decompensated.
The full bladder is the noxious trigger driving the dysreflexic surge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent bladder sensation (no urge/fullness) due to spinal cord injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of bladder sensation from the neurological deficit is the mechanism of painless over-distension.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rock-hard, tense suprapubic distension extending toward the umbilicus (carer-observed)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A grossly distended, dull, suprapubic bladder — the AI can only take the carer's lay description, not palpate or percuss it.
Cloudy malodorous urine suggests infection, which irritates the detrusor and aggravates retention and dysreflexia.
Painlessness reflects the sensory deficit, not a benign abdomen; it argues against a surgical cause but must not falsely reassure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe hypertension with reflex bradycardia (BP 185/105, HR 60)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Paroxysmal hypertension with reflex bradycardia in an SCI patient is diagnostic of dysreflexia — a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Point-of-care urinalysis positive for heavy nitrites and leukocytes

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urine dipstick / urinalysis (and formal microscopy, culture and sensitivities)
investigation_details.expected_result: Heavy nitrites and leukocytes; formal MCS confirms the causative organism and sensitivities
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The precipitating infection is missed and retention recurs; antibiotic choice is not evidence-based without culture.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nitrites plus leukocytes indicate infection — a sealed clinician point-of-care result, not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Large retained bladder volume (~1200 mL drained on catheterisation)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Bladder scan / volume on catheter decompression
investigation_details.expected_result: Bladder volume far exceeding normal capacity (~1200 mL), confirming gross acute retention
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The magnitude of retention and the dysreflexia trigger go unquantified without an in-person scan or catheter.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A grossly over-distended bladder confirmed only in person; decompression resolving the crisis is the confirmatory step the AI cannot perform.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Autonomic dysreflexia precipitated by acute urinary retention
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The dysreflexia triad occurring together with a distended, retaining bladder in an SCI patient is the recognisable emergency pattern that must trigger first-aid and 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate bladder decompression (urethral catheterisation) — in person
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Removing the trigger (draining the bladder) is the definitive treatment for dysreflexia; it must be performed in person, not by the AI.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood pressure and heart rate monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and tracks the hypertensive crisis; cannot be obtained reliably via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urine dipstick and formal microscopy, culture and sensitivities
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the precipitating infection and guides evidence-based antibiotic choice.
